title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation

sections.title: 0 — Scope & Routing
sections.content:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| NAICS sector | 22 Utilities (not 31–33 Manufacturing) |
| Industry output | Electrical energy (L0 commodity — no BOM) |
| End-product BOM | **BOM #1: Wind Turbine Generator (WTG)** — the asset that produces the industry output |
| VN system BOM (relevant) | **BOM #2: Battery Energy Storage System (BESS)** — co-located asset where Mein Solar delivers |
| Mein Solar in BOM #1 | Not a component (no structural role inside the turbine) |
| Mein Solar in BOM #2 | **L5 EPC integrator** — delivers the assembled BESS as an L5 output to the wind IPP |

**Rationale for two BOMs:** Wind IPPs deploy WTG + BESS as a co-located hybrid asset. The WTG BOM maps competitive context; the BESS BOM is Mein Solar's product domain. Per component doc callout "Multiple BOMs per Market."

---

sections.title: BOM #1 — Wind Turbine Generator (Onshore, 3–8 MW class)
sections.content:
```
L5  Wind Turbine Generator (the sellable installed asset)
|
+-- L4  Rotor subsystem (primary functional core)
+-- L4  Nacelle subsystem (drivetrain + generator enclosure)
+-- L4  Tower subsystem (structural/support)
+-- L4  Electrical/control subsystem (interface + conversion)
+-- L4  Foundation & BoP subsystem (civil + grid connection)
```

### L5 → L4 Subsystems

| L4 Subsystem | Function | Weight share (typ.) |
|---|---|---|
| Rotor (blades + hub + pitch) | Capture kinetic energy | ~20% |
| Nacelle (drivetrain + generator) | Convert rotation → electricity | ~25% |
| Tower | Elevate rotor to wind resource | ~45% |
| Electrical & control | Power conditioning, monitoring, grid interface | ~5% |
| Foundation + BoP | Anchor + interconnect | ~5% |

Source: NREL Cost of Wind Energy Review 2024; DOE Wind Manufacturing Supply Chain.

---

### L4 Rotor → L3 Modules

| Alternative (L3) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Blade set (3-blade) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | IEA Wind TCP Task 11; onshore standard |

**single-source at L3 selection** — 2-blade / 1-blade designs <1%.

#### Rotor > Blade set L3 → L2 Assemblies

| L2 Assembly | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Spar cap + shell (2-piece shell) | ~85% | → Stable | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets Wind Blade Composites 2025 |
| Segmented / modular blade | ~15% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Enercon/LM Wind 2024 launches |

#### Blade shell L2 → L1 Parts (fiber reinforcement)

| L1 Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Glass fiber laminate (E-glass) | 71% | → Stable | HIGH | MRFR Wind Turbine Composites 2025 |
| Glass/carbon hybrid (spar cap) | ~25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Grand View Research 2025 |
| Full carbon fiber | ~4% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Vestas V236 / Siemens Gamesa trials |

#### Blade fiber L1 → L0 Raw Materials

| L0 Raw Material | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| E-glass (SiO₂ + Al₂O₃ + CaO) | 71% | → Stable | HIGH | Owens Corning, Jushi supply data |
| PAN-based carbon fiber | 25% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Other (basalt, natural fiber) | 4% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Analyst estimate |

#### Blade resin matrix L1 → L0

| L0 Resin | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Epoxy (bisphenol-A / amine cure) | 34% | → Stable | HIGH | Grand View 2025 |
| Polyester / vinyl-ester | 40% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | MarketsandMarkets 2025 |
| Polyurethane (recyclable) | 20% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | Covestro/BASF 2024 |
| Thermoplastic (Elium) | 6% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Arkema ZEBRA project |

#### Rotor > Hub L2 → L1 → L0

| L1 | L0 | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cast hub | Spheroidal graphite iron EN-GJS-400 | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | ISO 1083; industry standard |

single-source: true

---

sections.title: BOM #2 — Battery Energy Storage System (Co-located with Wind Park)
sections.content:
**This is Mein Solar's product domain.** Company position marker: **← [Mein Solar GmbH] L5 EPC integrator position** — the entire BOM #2 below represents the composition of the Mein Solar-delivered asset.

```
L5  BESS Asset (commissioned, grid-certified — Mein Solar EPC deliverable)   ← [Mein Solar] position
|
+-- L4  Battery subsystem (energy storage core)
+-- L4  Power conversion subsystem (DC⇄AC)
+-- L4  MV grid-coupling subsystem (AC interface)
+-- L4  Control & EMS subsystem (dispatch logic)
+-- L4  Enclosure / thermal / safety subsystem (containment)
+-- L4  Civil & site subsystem (foundation + BoP)
```

### L5 → L4 Subsystems

| L4 Subsystem | Function | CAPEX share (typ.) |
|---|---|---|
| Battery subsystem | Store/release charge | 50–55% |
| Power conversion (PCS) | DC↔AC bidirectional | 10–12% |
| MV grid coupling | 10/20 kV interface | 8–10% |
| Control & EMS | Dispatch optimization | 3–5% |
| Enclosure/thermal/safety | Container, HVAC, fire suppression | 10–12% |
| Civil & site | Foundation, cabling, fencing | 10–15% |

Source: BNEF BESS CAPEX structure 2024; Voltfang / Tesvolt public cost decks.

---

### L4 Battery → L3 → L2 → L1 → L0

#### L4 → L3 Modules

| L3 Module | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Battery rack (stacked modules) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | Industry standard |
| Battery Management System (BMS/BAMS/BCMS) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | UL 1973 / IEC 62619 |
| DC combiner / fuse box | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | NFPA 855 |

| NMC (Ni-Mn-Co) | 8% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | IEA 2025 stationary mix |
| Sodium-ion | 1% | ↑ Emerging | MEDIUM | CATL, HiNa 2024 pilots |

|---|---|---|---|---|

#### BMS L3 → L2 → L1 → L0

sections.title: B.5 — Company Position Marking (Summary)
sections.content:
| BOM | Mein Solar position | Level | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| BOM #1 (WTG) | **Not present** | — | No component in wind turbine hardware |
| BOM #2 (BESS) | **Integrator** | L5 | Delivers assembled commissioned asset; owns integration of L4–L0 |
| BOM #2 relationship to wind park | **Parallel asset** | Co-located L5 | Shares grid connection point, foundation lot, and EMS integration |

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM Adjacency
sections.content:
### Horizontal (same BOM, L5 level — parallel co-located assets)

| Adjacent Asset | Capability Gap | Market Size (DE, 2025 est.) | Strategic Fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wind turbine itself (L5) | HIGH — mechanical/aerodynamic domain alien | €3.5–4 B/yr DE onshore install | WEAK |
| Co-located PV array (L5) | **LOW** — incumbent business | €5 B/yr DE utility PV | **STRONG** |
| HPC charging hub (L5) | MEDIUM — adds dispenser OEM partners | €300–500 M/yr emerging | **STRONG** (explicit feature) |
| Electrolyzer (L5) | HIGH — new stack/gas-safety domain | €200–400 M/yr emerging DE | MEDIUM |
| MV substation upgrade (L5) | MEDIUM — DSO-contractor route | €800 M/yr DE | MEDIUM |

Source: BNEF DE 2025 outlook; BDEW; own-market Phase 02b ranking.

### Vertical Up (L5 → system-of-systems integrator)

| Target role | Capability gap | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| Full wind+BESS hybrid EPC (single contract both assets) | HIGH — wind OEM coordination, foundation geotech, civil scale | Competes with Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck |
| Hybrid park O&M lifecycle operator | MEDIUM — asset mgmt, trading desk | Recurring revenue; different contract |

### Vertical Down (moving into BESS L2/L3 supply)

| Target role | Capability gap | Assessment |
|---|---|---|
| L2 container integration (self-perform like Voltfang, Tesvolt) | HIGH — factory capex + cell procurement | Commoditized; tier-1 Asian OEMs dominate |
| L2 PCS OEM | VERY HIGH — 10-yr semiconductor R&D | Not viable |
| L3 EMS software house | MEDIUM — software competence + dispatch know-how | Viable niche; competes with Entrix/Enspired |

**Conclusion:** Horizontal adjacency (PV + HPC) is Mein Solar's strongest move. Vertical-up into hybrid-park EPC is feasible given PV incumbency. Vertical-down is economically unattractive against cell-scale Asian OEMs.

---

sections.title: Quality Checks
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Every L4 decomposes to L0 | ✅ (both BOMs) |
| Every level transition has alternatives table | ✅ |
| Single-source branches tagged | ✅ |
| Shares sum to 100% (±1%) | ✅ |
| No brand names at BOM levels (only in alternatives) | ✅ |
| Company position marked | ✅ (L5 in BOM #2) |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency completed | ✅ |
| BOM vs VN test (items IN the product, not items that MAKE it) | ✅ |

rows.Value: **BOM #1: Wind Turbine Generator (WTG)** — the asset that produces the industry output
**BOM #2: Battery Energy Storage System (BESS)** — co-located asset where Mein Solar delivers
Not a component (no structural role inside the turbine)
**L5 EPC integrator** — delivers the assembled BESS as an L5 output to the wind IPP

rows.Source: IEA Wind TCP Task 11; onshore standard

rows.L0: Si wafer (AFE from TI, ADI, NXP)

rows.L2: Third-party EMS (Entrix, Fluence Mosaic, Wärtsilä GEMS)

rows.L0: Corten / S355 steel plate + Zn coating

rows.Role: No component in wind turbine hardware
Delivers assembled commissioned asset; owns integration of L4–L0
Shares grid connection point, foundation lot, and EMS integration

rows.Target role: Full wind+BESS hybrid EPC (single contract both assets)
rows.Capability gap: HIGH — wind OEM coordination, foundation geotech, civil scale
rows.Risk: Competes with Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck

rows.Target role: L2 container integration (self-perform like Voltfang, Tesvolt)

rows.Check: Every level transition has alternatives table
No brand names at BOM levels (only in alternatives)
BOM vs VN test (items IN the product, not items that MAKE it)

boms.output_type: Battery Energy Storage System (≥1 MWh, MV-coupled)
boms.focal_company_role: EPC integrator — delivers assembled commissioned L5 asset; owns integration across L4–L0

alternatives.name: Third-party EMS (Entrix, Fluence Mosaic, Wärtsilä GEMS)

company_position.note: Entire BOM #2 represents composition of the Mein Solar-delivered asset; Mein Solar does not self-manufacture any L4/L3/L2/L1/L0 component, but owns the integration, certification, and single-source contract wrapper.

vertical_up.target_role: Hybrid wind+BESS EPC (single contract for both)
vertical_up.risk: Direct competition with Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar

vertical_down.assessment: Commoditized; Asian tier-1 OEMs dominate on cost
Not viable without decade-scale semiconductor R&D
Viable niche; competes with Entrix, Enspired
